steps.action_description: Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Shred or chop the leftover chicken thighs. Measure out your spices into a small bowl.

meanwhile.action: Wash and pat dry the spinach if needed.

steps.action_description: In a large pot or deep skillet, melt the schmaltz over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until translucent (about 5 mins). Stir in the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

meanwhile.action: Get your cumin, coriander, and ginger ready.

steps.action_description: Add the cumin, coriander, and ginger powder to the pot. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until the spices smell toasted and aromatic. Be careful not to burn them!

meanwhile.action: Open the coconut milk and get the braise stock ready.

steps.action_description: Pour in the coconut milk and chicken stock, scraping any browned bits from the bottom of the pot. Stir in the frozen root vegetables and the chicken. Bring to a gentle simmer for 5-7 minutes until everything is hot and the sauce has slightly thickened.

meanwhile.action: PULL: Remove a small bowl of chicken and root veggies for kids if they aren't fans of 'saucy' greens.

steps.action_description: Stir in the fresh spinach and cook just until wilted (about 1-2 minutes). Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and top with a sprinkle of Aleppo pepper.
